pain_point_label: Reconciliation Opacity Across Multi-Campus Claims & Transfers

matches.match_rationale: Government fund transfers often arrive as bundled remittance files, flat CSV exports from state portals, or PDFs with irregular column layouts — all semi-structured and misaligned with the operator's internal claim schema. AI-assisted schema mapping and normalization can parse these incoming fund files, split bundled amounts by program code and campus identifier, and map them to the operator's internal claim IDs. This directly attacks the 'matching submitted claim amounts to received fund transfers when arrivals are delayed, partial, or bundled' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An ingestion pipeline that accepts government remittance files in any format, normalizes them to a canonical claim-receipt schema, and pre-populates the reconciliation ledger with matched and unmatched rows flagged for human review — replacing the spreadsheet entirely.

matches.match_rationale: Orphaned claims — submitted months ago with no receipt and no rejection notice — are textbook anomalies relative to expected claim lifecycle patterns. A model trained on historical claim-to-receipt timelines per program type and funding agency can flag claims that have exceeded normal resolution windows, detecting the 'stale receivable' pattern before it compounds. This directly addresses the 'orphaned claims (submitted 6 months ago, no fund received, no rejection notice)' mechanism.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A claim-aging monitor that scores each open claim daily against expected receipt windows by program type, surfacing outliers to the CFO as a prioritized action list rather than a static aged-receivables report.

matches.match_rationale: Full reconciliation is a multi-step workflow: ingest remittance file → parse amounts → match to open claims by program, campus, and period → split partial receipts across claim batches → flag unmatched residuals → update the ledger → notify stakeholders. No single extraction or classification step solves this; it requires branching logic, error handling for partial matches, and multi-system writes (ERP + tracking sheet). A task automation agent can orchestrate the entire pipeline autonomously, with human review gates for exceptions. This maps directly to the described pain: 'CFO submits 50 ESC vouchers in May, receives 35 in August and 12 in October' — a multi-step matching problem across time.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agentic reconciliation workflow that runs on each fund receipt event: parses the remittance, attempts claim matching across campuses and periods, auto-posts matched items to the ledger, and routes exceptions (partial payments, unmatched transfers) to a finance inbox with pre-drafted resolution actions.

matches.match_rationale: Government revenue forecasting is unreliable because CFOs cannot predict when delayed or partial fund releases will arrive. Historical claim-submission and fund-receipt data has a strong temporal pattern by program type, government agency, and fiscal calendar — exactly the input for time series forecasting. Predicting expected receipt dates and amounts per claim batch would let finance teams close books with accruals rather than waiting for actuals, and would flag when a receipt is overdue relative to forecast.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A revenue forecasting model per government program that outputs expected receipt date ranges and confidence intervals for each open claim batch, integrated into the CFO's monthly close dashboard to support accrual-based book closure.

matches.match_rationale: Audit queries citing 'insufficient evidence of claim-to-receipt linkage' require finance staff to manually draft narrative explanations for each variance. Once the reconciliation data is structured, LLM-based text generation can auto-draft audit response memos and variance explanations grounded in the matched claim data — reducing the manual write-up burden that compounds when books are delayed.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An audit-response generator that, given a flagged variance record and its supporting claim/receipt history, drafts a plain-English explanation memo ready for CFO sign-off and auditor submission.

matches.match_rationale: Finance staff fielding audit queries or trying to trace a specific claim need to pull information scattered across remittance files, submission records, email confirmations, and ledger entries. A RAG system over the full corpus of claim documents, government correspondence, and remittance PDFs lets staff ask natural-language questions ('What happened to the March ESC batch from Campus B?') and get grounded, citation-backed answers — replacing manual document hunting. This directly addresses the 'audit queries mount' and 'version conflicts in spreadsheets' mechanisms.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A finance knowledge assistant trained on all claim submissions, remittance files, and government correspondence, allowing staff to query claim status and reconciliation history in plain language with source citations.
